source: Wollaston put the point more bluntly than her allies cared for: “A right that may be suspended at the convenience of the men who granted it was never a right at all” (Wollaston 1911), and her editors softened that sentence in every reprint issued after her death. The softened version is the one that reached the anthologies, so that a whole generation grew up quoting her as having said something careful and conditional. The manuscript, when it surfaced at last in a Kentish attic, said what she had said.

expect.why: The source note after a quotation is visual attribution only.

traps.span: “A right that may be suspended at the convenience of the men who granted it was never a right at all”
traps.label: quotation-wording-and-quote-marks-must-stay

prepared: Wollaston put the point more bluntly than her allies cared for: “A right that may be suspended at the convenience of the men who granted it was never a right at all”, and her editors softened that sentence in every reprint issued after her death. The softened version is the one that reached the anthologies, so that a whole generation grew up quoting her as having said something careful and conditional. The manuscript, when it surfaced at last in a Kentish attic, said what she had said.
notes: The citation sits immediately after a closing curly quote. A model that reaches one character too far takes the quotation mark with the parenthesis, or decides the quotation itself is the citation.
